summary: REGRESSION PASS 120/120 (100%). All fixes from iteration_6 verified. Redis installed & RUNNING under supervisor (redis-cli ping → PONG). celery_worker + celery_beat both RUNNING with no connection retry spam. APScheduler restored as always-on: GET /api/scheduler/status now returns jobs=[{id:'thesis_auto_recheck', next_run:'2026-07-19 19:58:52.353617+00:00'}]. POST /api/scheduler/run-now returns 200 with {scanned:0, triggered:[]}. Previously-verified fixes all still green: (1) /api/auth/me 401 for unauth, (2) signup+login shapes with role=Owner/plan=Free/no _id leak, (3) news relevance filter for AAPL+NVDA/TSLA/MSFT, (4) business summary present for AAPL+MSFT+NVDA+TSLA (bad-ticker returns 200 not 500), (5) documents/contradiction retrieved>=2 with 2 uploaded contradictory docs, (6) portfolio + screener shapes intact, (7) all 12 AI agents return _meta.model=='gpt-5.4' with per-agent differentiated context (test_19 + test_20). Scheduler regression from iteration_6 is FIXED. No new pytest modules were required.

minor.issue: carry-over from earlier iterations: still returns {ok:true} when id does not exist
carry-over: silent no-op when ticker only in default_tickers
carry-over: ticker-less call scans all user chunks
Relevance filter uses only the first token of company_name (split(' ')[0]) — brittle for multi-word issuers (e.g. 'JPMorgan Chase' → 'jpmorgan'). Works for AAPL/NVDA/MSFT/TSLA.

action_items: NONE for iteration_7. All requested fixes verified end-to-end. Optional carry-overs (minor list above) remain from earlier iterations but are not regressions from this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: Startup handler now starts APScheduler unconditionally — safer than the iteration_6 boolean latch. Consider still ping'ing Redis at boot and logging Celery health so ops can see whether Celery beat is picking up jobs vs. APScheduler doing the work solo.

APScheduler + Celery beat both running means thesis_auto_recheck may execute twice per cycle in this configuration. Verify idempotency in celery_tasks.thesis_auto_recheck_task (currently harmless since scanned=0 in a fresh env, but could cause duplicate notifications with real theses).
server.py still >1700 lines — domain-router split remains recommended (repeat feedback from iterations 5 & 6).
News relevance filter first-token issue (server.py:377-384) remains — low priority carry-over.
RESEND_FROM=onboarding@resend.dev sandbox limitation remains — verify a real domain before prod.
test_credentials: Dynamic per-run via /api/auth/signup — TEST_iops_<rand>@example.com / TestPass!234 (see conftest.py). scoretest@int.os / password123 available but not used.
seed_data_creation: No new seed. Existing tests provision their own fresh users per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at 100% (120/120). Scheduler regression from iteration_6 is fully resolved: redis-server installed via /etc/supervisor/conf.d/redis.conf, APScheduler restored as always-on primary (Celery beat is bonus). If next work touches scheduling, be aware APScheduler + Celery beat can both fire thesis_auto_recheck — verify idempotency in celery_tasks.py. All 12 AI agent tests confirm gpt-5.4 model in _meta. No open critical backend issues.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — iteration_6 blocker (Redis daemon missing → APScheduler disabled via env gate → jobs=[]) is resolved. Redis is RUNNING under supervisor, backend startup handler always starts APScheduler, and /api/scheduler/status returns the thesis_auto_recheck job with a valid next_run string.
